changes_made: Changed k8s_networkpolicy_tls_enforcement to k8s_networkpolicy_tls_required for clarity
review_notes: The functions generally map well to Kubernetes security controls related to enforcing secure information flow. However, the function 'k8s_networkpolicy_tls_enforcement' was renamed to 'k8s_networkpolicy_tls_required' to better reflect the action of requiring TLS in network policies, aligning with naming conventions for clarity.
naming_improvements: Use 'tls_required' instead of 'tls_enforcement' for network policies to specify the requirement clearly
title: Information Flow Enforcement - Information flow enforcement | approved solutions employ [ assignment
step1_reasoning: The control AC-4(20) focuses on enforcing secure information flow using approved solutions. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring secure communication channels and enforcing TLS for data in transit. This involves enabling TLS for Ingress resources, ensuring mutual TLS (mTLS) in service meshes, enforcing TLS in NetworkPolicies where applicable, and ensuring the API server requires TLS client authentication.
